back_translation: Honored brothers and honored fathers, please listen to my defense which I will speak to you now.
When the crowd heard that he was speaking to them in Hebrew, they became even more quiet. Then Paul said,

I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in the province of Cilicia, but raised in this city, and I was educated at the feet of teacher Gamaliel according to the strict pattern of the Law of our ancestors. I had a fervent heart for God just as all of you are today,
I persecuted 'this Way' to the point of death, binding both men and women and sending them into prison,

as the chief priest and the whole council of elders bear witness for me. I also received letters from them to the Jewish brothers in the city of Damascus, then traveled there to bind those who were there and bring them back to Jerusalem to be punished.
While I was traveling and nearing the city of Damascus around noon, suddenly a brilliant light from heaven flashed around me.
I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?'
I answered, 'Who are you, Sir?' He spoke to me, saying, 'I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are persecuting.'
Those who were with me saw the light, but did not understand the voice of the One who was speaking to me.
I asked, 'What shall I do, Lord?' The Lord spoke to me, 'Get up and go into the city of Damascus; there someone will tell you everything that you have been appointed to do.'
I could not see anything because of the brilliance of that light, so those with me had to lead me by the hand into the city of Damascus.
A certain Ananias, a man who feared God and kept the Law strictly, was well-spoken-of among all the Jews living there,
came to me and stood beside me, then said, 'Saul, brother, look up!' At that very instant I could see him.
He said, 'The God of our ancestors has chosen you in advance to know his will, to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his royal mouth,
because you will be his witness to all people of what you have seen and heard.
Now, why are you delaying? Get up, receive baptism and wash away your sins, calling on his name.'
Later, when I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple precincts, I fell into a vision,
and I saw the Lord Jesus speaking to me, 'Hurry, leave Jerusalem quickly, because these people will not accept your testimony about me.'
I answered, 'Lord, they know very well that I used to imprison and beat all who believed in you in synagogue after synagogue.
And when the blood of Stephen, your witness, was being shed, I myself was also standing by, both approving and watching over the cloaks of those who were killing him.'
He spoke to me, 'Go! For I will send you to the Gentiles who are far away.'
The crowd listened to Paul up until this word, then lifted their voices and shouted, 'Eliminate such a person from the earth, for he ought not to be alive!'
While they were shouting, flinging off their cloaks, and tossing dust up into the air,
the commander ordered Paul to be brought into the barracks and ordered him to be interrogated by flogging, so that he might know why the crowd was shouting against him in that way.
While the soldiers were binding Paul with leather straps, he said to the centurion standing nearby, 'Is it lawful for you to flog a man who is a Roman citizen and has not had a trial?'
When the centurion heard that, he went and reported to the commander, 'What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.'
The commander then went to Paul and said, 'Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?' He answered, 'Yes, I am.'
The commander answered, 'I purchased this citizenship with a large sum of money.' Paul said, 'But I was born a Roman citizen by birth.'
Immediately, those who were about to interrogate Paul stepped back, and the commander himself became afraid when he learned that Paul was a Roman citizen, and that he had bound him in chains.

The next day, wanting to know the truth of what the Jews were charging Paul with, the commander had him released from the chains and ordered the chief priests and the whole supreme Council to assemble together. He brought Paul down and had him stand before them.
